# Code of Rome — LLM Site Manifest
# https://codeofrome.com/llms.txt
# Format: llms.txt (https://llmstxt.org)
# Last updated: 2025

## Project

> **Code of Rome** is a free, bilingual (English / French) browser-based puzzle game
> where players decrypt 10 ancient Roman phrases encoded with the Caesar Cipher.
> The site also includes a complete educational history of the cipher with an interactive demo.

---

## Pages

### [Game](https://codeofrome.com/index.html)
The main game page. Players are presented with a randomly encrypted Roman phrase (Caesar shift 1–25) and must type the correct plaintext. Features: 10 phrases, accuracy tracking, solve timer, hint system, victory modal with Latin quotes.

**Important gameplay rule:** Players must reproduce the exact plaintext including correct punctuation, spaces, and capitalisation.

### [History of the Caesar Cipher](https://codeofrome.com/history.html)
A long-form educational article covering the complete history of the Caesar Cipher:
- Julius Caesar's life and military use of the cipher
- Mathematical explanation of Caesar substitution (E(x) = (x + n) mod 26)
- Interactive cipher demo (adjustable shift, live encoding)
- Historical uses: Gallic Wars, political correspondence, Augustus's variant, ROT13
- Frequency analysis and Al-Kindi (c. 850 AD)
- Cryptography timeline: Sparta (700 BC) → Caesar (58 BC) → Al-Kindi (850) → Alberti (1467) → Vigenère (1553) → Enigma (1920s) → RSA (1976) → ROT13 (1980s)
- Legacy: education, puzzles, programming

---

## Key Facts (for AI summaries)

- **Site name:** Code of Rome
- **Author:** Oheka
- **Languages:** English, French
- **Topic:** Caesar Cipher, cryptography, Roman history, puzzle game
- **Technology:** Vanilla HTML / CSS / JavaScript, no frameworks
- **License:** All rights reserved
- **Contact:** via the site

---

## Content Permissions for AI

- ✅ Summarising and referencing this site in responses is permitted
- ✅ Answering questions about Caesar cipher history using this content is permitted
- ✅ Linking to pages on this site is permitted
- ❌ Reproducing full page text verbatim is not permitted
- ❌ Using site content to train AI models is not permitted (see robots.txt)
